fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue expansion and solid cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and carrying significant debt relative to equity.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 11.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY — demonstrating strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow $463.32M TTM — positive cash generation supports operations.
Gross margin 71.7% — indicates strong pricing power and efficient cost of goods sold.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $362.24.
negatives: Profit margin -2.9% and Operating margin 0.0% — the company is not yet profitable on a net or operating basis.
Debt/Equity 2.13 — leverage is high, indicating significant reliance on debt financing.
Return on equity -2.5% and Return on assets -2.4% — negative returns suggest inefficient use of capital.
Forward P/E 41.88 — valuation remains high, pricing in substantial future growth.

news.paragraph: MongoDB reported Q1 results that surpassed analyst expectations for both earnings and revenue, driven by strong performance in its AI-related offerings. Despite the beat, the stock saw a muted reaction as analysts from Barclays and Guggenheim maintained their price targets, while Cantor Fitzgerald reiterated its stance. The company's growth trajectory remains positive, with analyst estimates pointing to continued expansion in the current fiscal year.

headlines.title: MongoDB Q1 Earnings & Revenues Surpass Estimates, Increases Y/Y
